utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: donna torres , date of birth , 08/01/1980 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi donna ! how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm good . how about you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing well , thank you . and so , i saw that dr. brown put you on buspar . have you been on that before ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , that's new .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . how is it working for you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my anxiety is going good now , thankfully . i'm serious , it was brutal in november and december . finally , i was like , " i can not do this . " i have no idea why it happened . dr. ward did put me on singulair , and she did say we need to be careful because singulair can cause anxiety . so i'm not sure if that was the issue or what .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm . okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and it would , um , start usually during the day , at work .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i see .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i mean , i'm fine now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , that's good , that things have settled . i do wonder if some of what you are dealing with is hormonal , and that's why i was asking . 'cause you were on the progesterone , and i feel like you were having some irritability back then too .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and that was before we started the progesterone .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i know we started it for regulating your periods , but perhaps it helped with this also .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . and before , in november and december , i noticed that the week before my period , my anxiety would go through the roof . which then , i knew my period was coming . then it turned into my anxiety spiking just at random times .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmm , okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and it seemed like it was for no reason .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but november and december you were on the progesterone at that time .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so not really a link there , all right .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i do n't know .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i do n't know either . um , sometimes with the aging process , that can happen too .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i figured maybe that's what it was .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and we did go through the golive in november and december , so that can be pretty stressful also .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , and at work , that's when i first started to lead the process of delivering the results to patients with covid . in the beginning of the whole pandemic , patients would have to wait nine days before they'd get their results . and then we opened the evaluation centers and the covid clinic . so i think it just took a toll on me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , i can absolutely see that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , and then i was feeling selfish because i was n't even on the front lines . i mean , i was supporting people , sure , but i was n't in the icu . so i felt selfish and guilty . i mean , hands down , the physicians and nurses were in the thick of it and there i was , having anxiety . and it felt ridiculous .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , honestly , you feel how you feel and what you were doing was n't easy as well , so ... but let's see . i need to just put this dax back to work . all right , so no other issues whatsoever ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you lost weight ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , but i stopped taking the camila birth control . my hunger level was at a new high . i mean , i was eating constantly . i felt like , " what is going on ? "

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and now i am feeling better .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , that's good . and your masked face , though , it does look thinner .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , the past six months i have lost some weight .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . um , anything else going on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so your pap was in 2019 . i do n't think that we need to repeat that because it was negative/negative . um , have you ever had an abnormal pap ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not with you , but i did around 2009 , and then i had to be seen every six months for a while . and then i had a normal pap .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , well , let's just repeat it then .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that's fine with me , to be safe .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i know it sounds superstitious , but i feel like with all the immunocompromising , the pressure , the stress that people's bodies have been under , and the potential for getting covid or the vaccine ... i have actually seen some , um , an increase in abnormal paps in people who have been fine for a while . so that's why i figure let's just check .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . i fight the vaccine fight every day at home because my husband is n't ready to get it . same with my daughter . she shares the same worries as her dad in how it'll impact her when she gets older .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you had the vaccine ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i have . and so has my son . he , um , has had his first already .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , you know , you can only do what you can do .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i agree .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . well , let's complete your exam .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let's take a deep breath . and again . all right , you can breathe normally . all right , and take one more deep breath . okay , now i'm gon na touch your neck . go ahead and swallow . perfect . and just place your hand above your head . okay , i do feel some little bumps .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , but they're not as big as they were .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm . okay , in this breast it does feel a little bit denser . does it hurt at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it does , where your left hand just was .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , right here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , down here . but whenever i breastfed , it was always sore there too . i had a clog and something else . the lumps do feel smaller , but they are still there , unfortunately .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , they are . uh , well now i do n't know , because if it was the progesterone , they would've gone away .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , well just let your knees just op- relax and open . how's the itching or discomfort ? are you still using the cream ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , and i actually need to get that refilled for the first time ever .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , but yeah , i use it once a week and it does help .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . all right , looks good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you can go ahead and sit up .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so typically the lumps would often just shrink up pretty quickly after you've had one or two cycles , and you've had two cycles so far . so i think let's just keep monitoring them for now .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . and what could that mean ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , so just like people have an increased risk of breast cancer , there's also an increased risk for breast issues . you know what i mean ? so for example , cysts and lumps and fibroadenomas , those are all benign things . they're annoying and require some workup , but they're all benign .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and i'm- i'm just worried because i'm almost 40 and my mom was almost 45 when she was diagnosed with breast cancer . so i mean , i know there's nothing i can do about it , but it's just i feel like , uh , we had it under control and now it is n't .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , i would n't say that . i mean , i feel like we're at a point where we have a good cadence for you having surveillance on things , and i think you are more aware of your breasts than ever before , and things actually have n't changed .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so those are all good things .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: because , um , if it was cancer , we'd actually , we would see some change .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: we would ? okay , thank you for explaining that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . so i know it's annoying and distressing , but i think that's where we're at . it's annoying that you have the breast issue , and it's annoying that we have to follow them .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i agree there .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , but the only extra that i could po- , uh , potentially do , is we could get a breast specialist on the team and have you start to follow with them . and one of the advantages there is that they sometimes will do an ultrasound as an extension of their physical exam , in the office , to check out it- check it out on their own . uh , they also have a lot more experience and more willingness to sometimes perform procedures earlier , if they think it needs , um , if they think it needs to be done . and i think they tend to be much quicker than , you know , like radiology as to biopsy it .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . i'll do whatever you think i should .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . well , i think since you're feeling worried , let's go ahead and we can get them on board . i'll send out a referral and they will call you within the next couple of business days to schedule .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , i think that sounds great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . i do too . all right , well any questions or anything else we can discuss today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i think i'm all set .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , good . all right , well have a good rest of your day and just give us a call if you need anything else .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right , thank you . you have a good day too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 40-year-old female presenting for follow-up of bilateral breast cysts.
The doctor asks if the patient has previously taken Buspar.
The doctor asks how the patient is responding to Buspar.
The patient reports her anxiety is controlled on Buspar and notes she previously experienced severe anxiety in November and December.
The patient mentions she was prescribed Singulair and was warned it could cause anxiety.
The doctor suggests the patient's anxiety may be hormonal and notes the patient previously had irritability before starting progesterone.
The patient reports her anxiety previously spiked the week before her period and later became random.
The doctor notes that the aging process can contribute to anxiety.
The patient works in healthcare supporting Covid evaluation centers and clinics.
The doctor asks if the patient has lost weight.
The patient discontinued Camila birth control, which caused increased appetite, but she has since lost weight.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of abnormal pap smears.
The patient had an abnormal pap smear in 2009 requiring six-month follow-ups until normalization.
The doctor decides to repeat the pap smear.
The doctor asks if the patient has received the Covid vaccination.
The patient confirms she and her son have received the Covid vaccination.
The doctor performs a physical exam and notes the presence of breast lumps.
The doctor asks if the breast lumps are painful.
The patient reports the breast lumps are smaller but still present and sore.
The doctor asks about the patient's vaginal itching and use of cream.
The patient confirms she uses the vaginal cream once weekly and it helps with symptoms.
The doctor advises continuing to monitor the breast lumps.
The patient expresses concern regarding her family history of breast cancer, noting her mother was diagnosed at age 45.
The doctor recommends a referral to a breast specialist for further evaluation.
The doctor will send a referral for a breast specialist who will contact the patient to schedule.

case_description: follow-up bilateral breast cysts; anxiety; itchiness
